rationale: Verified the 2-file diff (21374bb8..389fcc41) is seam-transparent. internal/lifecycle/gitquery.go adds IsAncestor(workdir, ancestor, descendant string) (bool, error) and BranchExists(name string) bool as pure one-line delegations to gitutil.IsAncestor/BranchExists — identical signatures, no logic added. internal/approve/impl.go only repoints implIsAncestorFn and implBranchExistsFn to the new lifecycle wrappers and removes the internal/gitutil import; seam variable names, types, and injection points are unchanged, so every AC test that injects these seams is unaffected. Confirmed empirically: `go list -f '{{.Imports}}' ./internal/approve | grep gitutil` is empty (import removed); `go test -run TestEnforcementHasNoGitLeaks -v ./internal/lint/` PASSES (both subtests); `go test -count=1 ./internal/approve ./internal/lifecycle ./internal/complete ./internal/executor` all PASS (approve 45.5s, lifecycle 0.4s, complete 14.6s, executor 20.7s) with zero assertion changes. No import cycle: internal/lifecycle does not import internal/approve. Runtime behavior is byte-identical to round 1 (same gitutil functions execute at the bottom of the call chain) — my round-1 no-regression conclusion holds unchanged.
